text: Code refactoring is the process of improving the internal structure of code without changing its external behavior. The main goal is to make the code cleaner and easier to understand, which increases readability for developers. Common refactoring actions include renaming variables for clarity, splitting long functions into smaller ones, and removing duplicate code. By doing this regularly, teams can reduce technical debt—the hidden cost of writing messy code that becomes difficult to maintain over time. It's important to remember that refactoring is not about adding new features; it's simply about making the existing code better.
